mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals growing manufacturing output and economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months over a rolling 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above a threshold signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE triggers but SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below the year-over-year CPI inflation rate, real interest rates are negative, pushing investors toward equities as an inflation hedge and risk asset, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If real rates are negative but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months, the interaction mechanism is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the federal funds rate is high and still rising, higher discount rates suppress equity valuations and tighter financial conditions reduce economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the tightening regime is triggered but SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales indicate strong consumer demand, which drives revenue growth for companies and supports positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases but SPX 5-day returns are positive in less than 55% of weeks over a 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic slowdown, which reduces investor confidence and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST drops sharply but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real GDP and payroll employment confirm that the economy is expanding on both the output and labor fronts, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both GDPC1 and PAYEMS increase quarter-over-quarter
failure_condition: If both series increase but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production indicates a manufacturing recession that reduces corporate profits and economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines significantly but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
